description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular agricultural building with a steeply pitched gable roof, featuring overlapping, irregularly cut shingles in muted green and beige tones. The roof extends slightly beyond the walls, with exposed wooden beams at both gable ends, giving it a rustic appearance. The walls are constructed from dark brown wooden planks, reinforced with vertical support posts. At one end, there is a single, arched wooden door with visible metal hinges and a handle, suggesting a sturdy, functional entryway. The structure lacks visible windows, emphasizing its utilitarian design for livestock shelter. The overall form and materials evoke a traditional rural cowshed, suitable for placement in a farm or agricultural zone within an urban context.
description: A rustic, rectangular cowshed with a steep gable roof of green and beige shingles, dark wooden plank walls, and a single arched wooden door. The design is traditional and utilitarian, with exposed beams and no visible windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long wall with dark wooden planks, vertical supports, and the overhanging gable roof with layered green and beige shingles.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the arched wooden door with metal hinges and handle, and the full length of the steeply pitched roof.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the long wall and roof details without any visible entry points.
description_view_3: Rear end view showing a plain wall with no door or windows, and the gable roof's ridge line.
attribute_door_style: single arched wooden plank door with metal hinges and handle
